reasoning: The Lean statement restricts z to the unit circle via ‖z‖ = 1, and defines the partial sum s_n = ∑_{i ∈ range n} z^(i+1)/(i+1) = ∑_{k=1}^{n} z^k/k, which is precisely the partial sum of the series ∑_{n≥1} z^n/n (the natural starting index given the z^n/n term). The conclusion (∃ z₀, Tendsto s atTop (𝓝 z₀)) ↔ z ≠ 1 correctly captures "converges iff z ≠ 1", which matches "converges at every point of the unit circle except z = 1" (since at z = 1 the series becomes the divergent harmonic series). The indexing, the type (ℂ), and the iff structure all correctly encode the informal claim.
